rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice For New Hearing Date Re Defendant Motion: To Vacate Pre-Trial Sanction That Resuscitated Abated & Closed Violations To Enable Awards Of Dbi Penalties & Attorney Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 10, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANT ANNE KIHAGI Amended Notice For New Hearing Date Re Defendant Motion: To Vacate Pre-Trial Sanction That Resuscitated Abated & Closed Violations To Enable Awards Of Dbi Penalties & Attorney Fees is continued to December 15, 2021 to be heard with other pending motions. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SHANTEL MCCLENDON ET AL VS. THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Plaintiff'S First Amended Complaint Or, Alternatively, To Strike The Protected Portions Of Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: DEFENDANT THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY, CARLOS URIBE, ANNA CHUN Notice Of Motion To Strike Plaintiff'S First Amended Complaint Or, Alternatively, To Strike The Protected Portions Of Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 10, 2021 line 3. Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint is continued to January 12, 2022. No later than January 3, 2021 plaintiff to file a supplemental brief limited to list of factual allegations (with citations to complaint) of Defendant's wrongdoing that are not protected activity (e.g. service of Notice of Termination of Tenancy; filing an action for unlawful detainer) giving rise to each cause of action asserted in the complaint. For example (this is an example of format of the brief and this example is not based on complaint in this action): cause of action: breach of warranty of habitability; wrongful act/failure to act: landlord failed to fix a leaky faucet after notice on January 3, 2019 to present; citation: Complaint, paragraph 18, page 9, lines 7-13. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: MB PROPERTY GROUP VS. MARINA PERLS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Judgment Against Defendant Due To Defendants Excusable Neglect And Attorney Error (C.C.P. 473(B)); Notice Of Motion; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Defendant Iso; Declaration Of Asma Husain Iso; Proposed Order
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 10, 2021 line 5. DEFENDANT YULIY KOBRINSKIY Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Judgment Against Defendant Due To Defendants Excusable Neglect And Attorney Error (C.C.P. 473(B)) is DENIED. Judgment was entered on November 5, 2021 based on ex-parte application for which both parties appeared. Arguments in opposition were presented and considered by the Court as evidenced by the Court's order and judgment finding that defendant was in breach of the stipulation and providing the defendant additional time to comply (due to the arguments made in opposition). Thereafter, Defense counsel read only one of the two documents issued by the Court on November 5, 2021. This Court always issues an order granting application and a judgment (if the application requested an entry of judgment). Counsel does not explain why the second document was not read. This failure constitutes neglect contemplated by CCP 473(b). However, even though counsel admits to finding out about the Court's extended deadline to comply with the stipulation on November 11, 2021, this motion was not filed until November 24, 2021. As of November 24, 2021, it does not appear that Defendant has complied with the requirement under the stipulation to apply for rental assistance. Even if the Court recalls the writ that was based on failure to comply with the extended deadline, Defendant would still remain in breach of the stipulation and the Court order. Since this request for relief is futile, the motion is DENIED. Defendant and his counsel are ordered to comply with SFLR 2.7B in future. No courtesy copies of the moving papers received by the Court for this motion (for the original hearing date of December 27 or the advanced hearing date). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
